task_completion.description: Create at least two interior partition walls.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D floor plan or 3D view in Sweet Home 3D for at least two newly drawn interior walls that create an enclosed room (e.g., staff room) or separate a specific area (e.g., cafe prep area).

task_completion.description: Furnish the Retail Zone with at least 10 bookcases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count the bookcase or shelving units placed in the retail area. There must be at least 10 visible in the 2D plan or 3D view.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Retail Zone with at least one checkout counter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a desk, counter, or cash wrap furniture item placed in the retail area, intended for customer checkout.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Cafe Zone with at least three tables, six chairs, and one appliance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the cafe area in the 2D plan or 3D view. Verify there are at least 3 tables, at least 6 chairs (usually arranged around the tables), and at least 1 appliance (e.g., coffee maker, refrigerator, oven, or microwave).

task_completion.description: Identify the areas with at least three text labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least three distinct text labels added to the 2D floor plan identifying the new zones (e.g., 'Retail', 'Cafe', 'Staff Room', 'Prep Area').

task_completion.description: Document main aisle widths with at least two dimension lines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least two dimension lines (lines with measurement numbers, e.g., '120 cm' or '4 ft') drawn across the aisles between bookcases or tables on the 2D floor plan.
Look for the 'Save' dialog being interacted with, a 'File saved' notification, or the application title bar showing the filename without an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk).

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical interface to complete the design.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application open, with the agent actively dragging and dropping items from the catalog, drawing walls, and using the UI tools, rather than running terminal scripts to modify the file.

integrity.description: The agent placed actual 3D furniture models and walls.
integrity.visual_evidence: The bottom 3D view pane in Sweet Home 3D must accurately reflect the 3D models of the walls, bookcases, tables, chairs, and appliances placed in the top 2D plan.

integrity.description: The agent worked within the provided starter file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The layout should be built inside the original blank commercial shell provided at the start of the task, not in a completely different pre-made residential or commercial project file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires the agent to modify the 'bookstore_starter.sh3d' file, which is a blank commercial shell. The agent must use Sweet Home 3D's tools to add partition walls, place specific quantities of furniture (10+ bookcases, 1+ checkout counter, 3+ tables, 6+ chairs, 1+ appliance), and use the annotation tools to add 3+ text labels and 2+ dimension lines. The starter file may retain some baseline walls/labels/dimensions, so focus on verifying the presence of the newly required elements for the bookstore and cafe layout.
